Noel Ayala Gallo is originally from Harrisonburg, VA. He is an aspiring lawyer and is of Cuban and Salvadoran descent.

Isaac Antony Rodríguez, PhD, PhD, is the Co-Founder and Chief Science Officer of SweetBio, a commercial biotechnology company transforming wound recovery. He is a co-inventor of SweetBio’s FDA-cleared, insurance-reimbursed collagen and honey wound products. SweetBio graduated from the Memphis-based ZeroTo510 Medical Device Accelerator in 2015 and has since raised $10M, secured two FDA clearances, received patents in 19 countries, completed a randomized controlled clinical trial, and become revenue-generating. The company has been featured by Apple, 60 Minutes, USA Today, Forbes, Inc., and PEOPLE en Español.
Beyond SweetBio, Dr. Rodriguez is deeply committed to community leadership and serves on the boards of the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers, the Virginia Latino Higher Education Network, Seeding Success, New Memphis Institute, and the Collegiate School of Memphis. He was a keynote speaker for NASA Langley’s 2017 Hispanic Heritage Month Celebration and received the SHPE STAR Entrepreneur Award in 2023.

Cristian Hernandez is a project management professional with over 10 years of experience in financial services and stakeholder engagement. He currently works in Single-Family Market Engagement under the Front Office team at a Fortune 50 government-sponsored enterprise, supporting initiatives that strengthen market relationships and drive impact.
He holds a bachelor’s degree from the University of Virginia in Latin American Studies and Spanish. His professional background includes personal banking, debt counseling, collections, and customer and partner management. Proud of his Latin American roots, Cristian values service, mentorship, cultural awareness, and lifelong brotherhood.
